events.text: Findings written to `/app/answer.json`. Summary: bend radius violations on data/comm/security cabling are an **installed-defective/non-compliant** condition — kinked cable at corners, staples, and panel entries tightens the bend below TIA-568's minimum (≈4× cable OD for UTP), risking degraded return loss/NEXT/attenuation or permanent twist-geometry damage. Since it's only rough-complete, decision is **fail** (medium risk) with corrective re-routing/re-dressing and re-certification required before cover-up sign-off.
